records.assistantPreview: The Clerk holds the cream sheet between two ink-dark fingers, copper edge catching lamplight, but Mira's gaze has already moved—past the woman's shoulder, past the pigeonholes, to where the back wall meets a seam of darker air. A service do

The vendor's hands pause over a stack of folded canal charts—weathered fingers, ink crescents under every nail, a brass coin-clasps glinting at the cuff of his sleeve. He's older than Mira first guessed, the kind of lean that comes from yea

The claim falls flat. Mira's hand closes around cold brass and nothing else—no ring of keys, no weight of authority, just the latch and a lie that the air doesn't believe.

But the door gives anyway.

Not from any master key. The lacquer-me

Mira steps back through the doorway, into the alcove again—retreating into the wax-heavy air of the stamp room while the open door gapes behind her like a confession.
